ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for mock chicken makhani. FOODON_03311069 (curry dish) is the nearest defensible parent category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKN (dishes based on meat substitutes) is the closest defensible parent. No exact code exists for mock chicken makhani specifically.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for mock chicken makhani or vegetarian butter chicken curry.

main_ingredients: mock chicken (soy protein or textured vegetable protein)

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical restaurant serving of mock chicken makhani is approximately 300 g, comprising a generous portion of mock chicken pieces in makhani (butter-tomato-cream) gravy, usually served with rice or bread.

nutrient_source.title: Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) — ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods; supplemented with USDA FoodData Central soy protein analogue data for mock-meat component
nutrient_source.organization: ICMR-NIN (Indian Council of Medical Research – National Institute of Nutrition); USDA FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct entry for mock chicken makhani exists in ICMR-NIN or USDA. Values are adapted from ICMR-NIN butter chicken / makhani gravy data with the chicken protein component replaced by textured soy protein analogue (USDA FDC NDB 16120 / similar). Energy and macros adjusted proportionally. Fat is slightly lower than traditional murgh makhani due to absence of chicken fat; protein is comparable due to soy TVP. Sodium estimated from ICMR-NIN makhani gravy data. This is a medium-confidence adapted estimate.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of mixed Indian curry dishes — analogous estimate based on tomato-cream-based curry with legume/soy protein; reference: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for mock chicken makhani. GI estimated at 45 (low) based on analogous tomato-cream-based Indian curry dishes with soy/legume protein from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 international GI tables. The makhani sauce is tomato and cream based with low net carbohydrate density; soy protein has a low GI (~25). Composite GI estimated at 45. Glycemic load per 300 g serving = (45 × 7.2 × 3) / 100 = 9.72, rounded to 9.7.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~320 mg/100 g; ~960 mg per 300 g serving)
hypertension.guidance: A 300 g restaurant serving provides approximately 960 mg sodium (~42% of the 2300 mg daily limit), so individuals with hypertension should limit portion size, request reduced-salt preparation, and avoid pairing with other high-sodium accompaniments such as pickles or pappadums.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (~45) and moderate carbohydrate content (~7.2 g/100 g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Mock chicken makhani has a low estimated GI of approximately 45 and a glycemic load of ~9.7 per 300 g serving, making it a relatively suitable choice for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed with a low-GI staple such as basmati rice or whole-wheat roti and portion-controlled to manage total carbohydrate intake.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from cream and butter (~3.2 g/100 g; ~9.6 g per serving)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The cream and butter in the makhani sauce contribute approximately 9.6 g of saturated fat per 300 g serving (~48% of the 20 g daily limit for a 2000 kcal diet), so individuals with cardiovascular disease should request reduced-cream preparation or limit portion size to manage saturated fat intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Mock Chicken Makhani (also known as Vegetarian Butter Chicken or Murgh Makhani Vegetarian) is an Indian vegetarian dish that replicates the flavour profile of the classic North Indian butter chicken (murgh makhani) using mock chicken made from textured soy protein or other plant-based meat substitutes in place of real poultry. The dish is served at Indian vegetarian restaurants in Singapore, including Jewel of India at Boat Quay. The makhani sauce is a rich, mildly spiced tomato-cream-butter gravy seasoned with garam masala, cumin, coriander, turmeric, ginger, garlic, and kasuri methi (dried fenugreek leaves), giving it a characteristic orange-red colour, velvety texture, and a mildly sweet, tangy, and aromatic flavour. The mock chicken pieces absorb the sauce and provide a chewy, meat-like texture. Nutritionally, the dish provides moderate protein (~7.5 g/100 g) from soy, moderate fat (~6.8 g/100 g) primarily from cream and butter, and low carbohydrate (~7.2 g/100 g). The estimated glycemic index is approximately 45 (low), making it relatively suitable for blood glucose management. Sodium content is moderate to high (~320 mg/100 g), which is a consideration for individuals with hypertension. The dish is suitable for lacto-vegetarians and is typically halal. It is commonly served with basmati rice, naan, or roti and is a popular choice for those seeking a plant-based alternative to a beloved North Indian classic.
common_user_queries: What is mock chicken makhani made of?
How many calories in mock chicken makhani?
Is mock chicken makhani suitable for diabetics?
What is the GI of vegetarian butter chicken?
Is mock chicken makhani high in sodium?
Can I eat mock chicken makhani if I have high blood pressure?
What is the difference between mock chicken makhani and regular butter chicken?
Where can I find mock chicken makhani in Singapore?
meal_context: paired with basmati rice or naan

confidence_reason: No direct nutritional entry for mock chicken makhani exists in ICMR-NIN or USDA FoodData Central. Values are adapted from ICMR-NIN butter chicken / makhani gravy data with the chicken component replaced by soy TVP analogue data from USDA FDC. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008. Confidence is medium due to the adapted nature of the nutrient data and the absence of a direct GI measurement for this specific dish.
notes: 1. Food name corrected from 'Mock Cbiken Makhni' (apparent OCR/transcription error in source data) to 'Mock Chicken Makhani (Vegetarian)' as the canonical English name. 2. Cuisine field in input contained the full restaurant name; corrected to 'Indian Vegetarian'. 3. Nutrient values are adapted estimates; a direct laboratory analysis or HPB/ICMR-NIN entry for this specific dish would improve confidence to high. 4. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as this nutrient is not reliably reported for plant-based mock-meat curry dishes in ICMR-NIN or USDA FDC for this food category. 5. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as this nutrient is not reported in the ICMR-NIN source for makhani-type dishes. 6. selenium_mcg is -1 as selenium is not reported in the ICMR-NIN source for this food category. 7. GI is an analogous estimate at 45 (low) based on tomato-cream-based Indian curry dishes with soy/legume protein from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008; no direct GI measurement exists for mock chicken makhani. 8. The dish is served at Jewel of India, an Indian vegetarian restaurant at Boat Quay, Singapore (GrabFood listing, 1 restaurant). 9. Price: SGD 16.80 (single restaurant, single price point). 10. Multilingual names provided for Singaporean context (English, Chinese Simplified, Chinese Traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) as the dish is served in Singapore.
